--- Commit Message ---
LU-17327 tests: add test case for online MDT/OST addition

Add conf-sanity test_46b for adding MDTs and OSTs online
to a live Lustre filesystem that is under load.

Alternate adding MDTs and OSTs until the config limit is reached,
while copying directory trees into two different mountpoints.
There should be no errors hit by rsync, and the new MDTs and OSTs
should (eventually) be used by the copied directories.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=46b,ONLY_REPEAT=5 mdtcount=8 mdscount=4
Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=46b,ONLY_REPEAT=20 mdtcount=4 mdscount=2
Change-Id: Ib471c76ffffb0205ce2eee5d1e43867566eb2f41
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2023-12-08 22:07:03.000000000) ---
Patch Set 19:

The ZFS -ENOSPC failures look like "real" out of space errors on the server, or possibly a grant issue.  I found messages in the ZFS debug log showing the client was sending 1-page sync writes, meaning it didn't get any grant, and the OSD was still returning -ENOSPC during the write.

However, there was also an error in the test script that was supposed to reduce the amount of data copied to ZFS filesystems (I had "avail /= 2" instead of "limit /= 2") so possibly this will be more reliable now.

I've also made the enhanced "SLOW=y" testing the default for Janitor testing, on the hopes of getting an actual debug log that shows the -ENOENT case.  Usually this is long gone from the logs by the time they are dumped.

Patch Set 43:

It looks like it isn't possible to tune the grant_shrink_interval for the osp devices. Instead I've reduced the MDT count so that fewer files will be created, which should avoid the OOS. There haven't been any signs of MDT addition failing causing failures in any of the test runs, only the grant usage (which is unrealistic in real life due to the very small OST sizes).
